channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
Summer Game Fest 2026 is one of the most intriguing showcases in a while, as the slate of games on the horizon starts to become clear, all while tussling to avoid the looming behemoth of Grand Theft Auto 6.

The PlayStation State of Play showcase earlier this week showed how wide a berth titles are giving to GTA 6, avoiding its November release date, and, as a result, September 2026 and February 2027 are already looking busy—and there are still some big reveals to come.

Unlike State of Play, the Summer Game Fest 2026 showcase features titles across all platforms, and the two-hour show is set to be packed with reveals, world premieres, and plenty more.

All Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase Announcements

As games are shown, they will be updated below in the order they appeared. If something is missing, please let us know in the comments. Trailers will also be added as they become available.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

Paramount Unveils New Game Studio; First Reveal Coming Today

Skydance Interactive and Skydance New Media have been merged into one to create Paramount Games Studio. Here are more details.

No Rest for the Wicked XBOX Release Delayed Due to Series S

No Rest for the Wicked’s XBOX release has been delayed due to Series S optimization issues. Here are more details.

Escape from Tarkov: Arena Bug Inspired New CheckPoint: Knives Out Mode

Battlestate Games has added a new mode to Escape from Tarkov: Arena named CheckPoint: Knives Out, which adds a melee-only event.

New Hot Wheels Game ‘Infinite Rush’ Release Date and Trailer Revealed

A new Hot Wheels game, Infinite Rush, is in development, with details on the release date and trailer revealed in a new report.



https://insider-gaming.com/summer-game-fest-2026-showcase-everything-announced/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Everything Coming in Rocket League Season 23
item.content:encoded:
Rocket League Season 23 is coming soon and is going to be themed around the upcoming football World Cup.

Epic Games and Psyonix have released full details about what Rocket League fans can look forward to in the upcoming update.

There is a lot of buzz surrounding the game following the RLCS Paris Major and the announcement of Unreal Engine 6.

As well as World Cup cosmetics, the update is also bringing huge quality-of-life improvements to the game, including side-selection after a demo.

Here is everything new coming in Rocket League Season 23.

Rocket League Season 23 Release Date

Rocket League Season 23 will release on Wednesday, June 10 at 9:00 AM PT (12:00 PM EDT).

Rocket League World Cup Content

Unlike EA Sports FC 26’s World Cup mode, Epic Games has secured the FIFA World Cup 2026 licensing for Rocket League.

The Rocket League x FIFA World Cup 2026 limited-time event (LTE) will run until July 20, 9 AM PT.

It will also see the release of a World Cup-themed Arena Variant called United Futura.

Once Season 23 releases, players will be able to pick their nation by July 13 9:00 AM PT (12:00 PM EDT) to claim a free Octane Decal and Title.

This decal will track players’ performances for a global leaderboard.

Once the World Cup ends, every player will receive a “World Cup Champion” Player Title, with the winning country’s flag, and all 47 other World Cup Decals.

Players who played for the winning country will also receive a gold variant of the title.

Additionally, LTE Challenges will reward a Goal Explosion, a Boost, and a universal Decal celebrating the football tournament.

Completing 10 out of the 14 World Cup Challenges will then unlock the Hyundai Ioniq 6 Car Body.

Rocket League Quality of Life Upgrades

As well as the World Cup content, Psyonix is also adding a ton of quality-of-life improvements to the 10-year-old game.

In possibly one of the biggest changes ever, Rocket League will now allow players to choose what side they respawn on.

The total list of new features coming in Rocket League Season 23 are:

  • Demo Respawn Control
  • Custom Training Velocity and Car Pitch, and Local Starting Boost Control
  • Ball and Car Speed Graphs
  • Streamer Anonymizer
  • Hitbox Visualization
  • Auto-Skip Goal Replays
  • Improved Friend Presence to include score and time remaining 
  • Goal Scorer’s View in Replays

Rocket League Jynxzi Event

Rocket League is also partnering with content creator Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart for a mini event.

Those who end up in a match with the popular streamer will receive Jynxzi-themed cosmetics.

If you win on the same team as Jynxzi, you’ll get the Dad’s Reinforced Chair. Defeat him, and you’ll get the Jynxzi’s OG Chair Topper.

Win or Lose, you’ll also receive the BAZALRIGHT Decal. 

Rocket Pass Premium

Season 23’s Rocket Pass Premium will unlock two new vehicles:

  • •Ryza Trophy (Octane Hitbox) – first unlock
  • •Ryza T60  (Breakout Hitbox) – in the higher Tiers

The Pass also features new Boosts, new Trails, fresh Antennas, and Player Anthems built for the season.

What other features would you like to see added to Rocket League? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And relive the Paris Major Grand Final as Vatira furthered his claim of Rocket League GOAT.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/everything-coming-in-rocket-league-season-23/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: KRIMZ Leaves Fnatic Counter-Strike Roster After 12 Years
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic have announced that its Counter-Strike 2 (CS2) team is parting ways with its longest-serving player, Freddy ‘KRIMZ’ Johansson.

The Swede originally joined Fnatic in 2014 and spent a total of 12 years across two stints with the organisation, but has now decided to leave.

KRIMZ spent a total of 4,288 days with Fnatic, barely missing out on the record for the longest-ever consecutive tenure on one team because of a two-month stint on GODSENT in 2016.

Fnatic’s remaining CS2 roster is now made up entirely of Ukrainian players.

It’s expected that another Ukranian will be added to Rodyon ‘fear’ Smyk, Dmytro ‘jambo’ Semera,Mykyta ‘jackasmo’ Skyba, and Viktor ‘Br4tkO’ Kondratets.

KRIMZ’s Fnatic Farewell

KRIMZ shared an emotional message on social media following the announcement that he is leaving, admitting it was a “tough decision.”

“A massive thank you to Fnatic for believing in me, supporting my growth, and making my childhood dream a reality. Going from watching my idols as a kid to actually sharing the jersey with them is more than I ever could have imagined.”

He then thanked Fnatic fans and told them to remain positive about the remaining core of CS players:

“I know it’s been a rough ride lately, but I want you to know that we showed up and put the work in every single day. Sometimes things don’t go your way, but I can honestly say I gave it everything I had until the very last day.

“I’m leaving with my head held high and I’ll always look back on my time at Fnatic knowing I left everything out there.”

Within his 12 years on the organisation, KRIMZ recorded quite a collection of accolades and achievements, as shared by Fnatic:

  • 2414 Maps played for Fnatic
  • 4282 Days in Black and Orange
  • 2x Major Titles
  • 21 notable trophies
  • 3x IEM Katowice titles
  • 5x HLTV Top 20 appearances
  • Longest-serving player in Fnatic History

It’s not yet known where KRIMZ will end up next. He has had an impressive career, but his recent form hasn’t been the best.

Who do you expect to replace KRIMZ? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, Passion UA is reportedly open to selling its players.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #824 Hints and Answers for Today, June 5, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry; below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #824 for Friday, June 5, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints for Today, June 5, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #824 are all terms related to fighting.

General Strands Hints for June 5

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: The Super Smash Bros. game on Nintendo GameCube.
  • Hint 2: The Super Smash Bros. game on Nintendo Wii.
  • Hint 3: WWE’s most recent Premium Live Event was ___ in Italy.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #824 for June 5, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for June 5, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FIGHT
  • SALE
  • RANG
  • SEED
  • REEL

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #824 On June 5, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • SCUFFLE
  • MELEE
  • BRAWL
  • SCRAP
  • CLASH
  • BRAWL
  • SKIRMISH
  • SPANAGRAM: FIGHTINGWORDS

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #824?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on June 6 will be Strands Game #825.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android
